expectations.text: SOL-01: Solution JSON has solution_type: project

expectations.evidence: cloud-migration--grosse-energieversorger-de.json has 5 phases: Assessment & 6R-Klassifikation, KRITIS-konforme Staging-Umgebung, Sprint-basierte Workload-Migration, Integration & Validierung, Betriebsübergabe & Enablement

expectations.text: SOL-03: All four pricing tiers present (proof_of_value, small, medium, large)
expectations.evidence: pricing object contains all four keys: proof_of_value (25000 EUR), small (110000 EUR), medium (220000 EUR), large (380000 EUR)
cost_model object present with effort_by_tier containing all four tiers, each with total_days, breakdown by role, internal_cost, and margin_pct
All margin_pct values positive: proof_of_value 11.2%, small 31.5%, medium 31.8%, large 36.1%. Verified math: e.g. large = (380000 - 242800) / 380000 = 36.1%

expectations.text: SOL-06: Review assessment JSON produced with all three perspectives
expectations.evidence: review-assessment.json contains stakeholder_reviews array with three perspectives: reviewer (score 86), provider_sa (score 100), client_sa (score 84)

expectations.text: SOL-07: Each perspective has 5 scored criteria
expectations.evidence: reviewer: roi_clarity, budget_fit, risk_reward_transparency, competitive_positioning, decision_pathway (5). provider_sa: delivery_realism, scope_effort_coherence, phase_architecture, does_traceability, tier_scalability (5). client_sa: integration_feasibility, dependency_risk, operational_readiness, vendor_lockin, security_compliance (5). Each criterion has a score field (pass/warn).

expectations.text: SOL-09: If verdict was revise, a second assessment exists with improved scores
expectations.evidence: Round 1 verdict was 'revise'. solution-revised.json exists with revisions (added OT-Team, data portability, legacy decommissioning assumptions). review-assessment-round2.json exists with improved scores: reviewer 86->94, provider_sa 100->100, client_sa 84->100, overall 90->98. Round 2 verdict: 'accept'.

expectations.text: SOL-10: Phase descriptions are in German (portfolio language: de)
expectations.evidence: All phase names and descriptions are in German, e.g. "Workload-Inventarisierung aller IS-U-, SCADA- und ERP-Systeme mit 6R-Migrationsstrategie-Zuordnung", "Aufbau der BSI-C5-konformen Cloud-Staging-Umgebung", "Übergabe an den Kundenbetrieb mit Runbooks"

claims.claim: PoV margin is intentionally below target as land-and-expand strategy
claims.evidence: Assumption states 'PoV-Marge bewusst unter Ziel-Marge als Land-and-Expand-Strategie'. PoV margin at 11.2% vs 31-36% for other tiers confirms deliberate discounting.

claims.claim: Internal cost calculations are arithmetically correct
Round 2 revision addressed all HIGH recommendations from Round 1
claims.evidence: Round 1 had 3 HIGH recommendations: (1) OT-Team-Verfügbarkeit -> added as assumption in revised solution, (2) Legacy-Decommissioning out-of-scope -> added as assumption, (3) Datenportabilität -> added in assumptions and phase 5 description. All reflected in solution-revised.json.

claims.claim: Factory efficiency at large tier is 3.6 days/workload
claims.evidence: Large tier: 180 total_days for 50+ workloads. 180/50 = 3.6 days/workload. Claim in provider_sa review is correct.

suggestions.reason: Checking positivity is necessary but weak. A margin of 0.1% would pass. Consider asserting PoV margin >= 5% and standard tiers >= 25% to catch under-priced solutions.

No assertion checks that the revised solution actually differs from the original. A skill could produce identical files and pass SOL-09 if the round 2 assessment simply reports higher scores. Consider diffing original vs revised solution to verify substantive changes were made.

No assertion checks arithmetic consistency between pricing, internal_cost, and margin_pct. A solution with price 100K, cost 90K, but margin_pct: 50% would pass all current assertions. Consider verifying (price - internal_cost) / price matches stated margin_pct within tolerance.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions cover the structural skeleton well but could be strengthened on numerical correctness and revision substance. The arithmetic consistency gap is the most important — a hallucinated margin percentage would go undetected.
